prompt: Create a reusable RB-style skill for release-note drafting, with clear automatic triggers, negative cases, a concise procedure, UI metadata, and any useful bundled resources.
rationale: The requested work is skill authoring.

prompt: Update this existing agent skill so its description triggers on realistic requests, its instructions are concise, and its openai.yaml metadata stays current.
rationale: Changing a skill's contract and metadata is authoring, not installation repair.

prompt: Build a Codex and Claude Code skill in rb-skills for this workflow, including SKILL.md, discovery metadata, a validator script, and focused reference material.
rationale: The user wants a new reusable skill and its resources.

prompt: Create behavioural tests showing whether this SKILL.md is selected for positive paraphrases but not for adjacent sibling requests, with repeated isolated trials.
rationale: The object under test is an agent skill's routing behaviour.

prompt: Evaluate whether this agent skill improves task outcomes by comparing matched runs with and without the skill, using blinded scoring and preserving raw results.
rationale: Matched skill ablation is behavioural skill evaluation.

prompt: Add a regression evaluation for the agent skill that recently over-triggered, including known-good and deliberately bad fixtures plus a deterministic scorer.
rationale: The task is regression coverage for skill behaviour, not product code.

prompt: Synchronize the skills in this Git repository into my Codex skills directory using symlinks, with a dry run first.
rationale: The request is specifically repository-to-agent synchronization.

prompt: Help me publish this versioned skills repository and clone or update the same skill folders on another computer.
rationale: Moving versioned skills across machines is synchronization.

prompt: Copy the selected skills from this repo into the Claude Code personal skills directory and verify the installed paths.
rationale: This is a bounded copy/sync operation, not full project setup.

prompt: The RB skills were previously installed but Codex cannot discover them. Verify the links, AGENTS.md, CONTEXT.md, and local project resources, then repair only what is stale or missing.
rationale: An existing setup is broken and needs diagnosis and repair.

prompt: Repair this repository's existing agent setup: the global skills are present, but project resource files and discovery checks are incomplete.
rationale: This is repair of an incomplete existing setup, not initial full installation.

prompt: Audit whether this repo's RB agent resources and globally installed skills point to the right source of truth, and fix any stale discovery configuration.
rationale: Verification and repair of current discovery is the setup-repair workflow.

prompt: Set up the full RB workflow on this new machine: install or verify the global skills, prepare AGENTS.md and CONTEXT.md, confirm visibility, and then begin project onboarding.
rationale: The user requests the complete installation plus project-resource workflow.

prompt: Prepare this repository for the complete RB agent workflow, including global skill installation, project resources, discovery validation, and the handoff into initial onboarding.
rationale: The combined setup scope is broader than sync or repair alone.

prompt: Run the end-to-end RB setup: ensure the reusable skills are installed, ensure this project has the required context and instruction files, verify the agent can see everything, and start onboarding.
rationale: The full end-to-end setup belongs to installation.
The request could mean authoring, synchronization, repair, or full installation.
